introduction.p: Open a browser and point it to Desmos. In Desmos, enter q(x) = ax^2 + bx + c; you will be prompted to add sliders for a, b, and c. Do so. Then begin exploring with the sliders and respond to the following questions.

statement.p: Describe how changing the value of a affects the graph of q.

solution.p: If a > 0 the parabola opens upward; if a < 0 it opens downward. The larger |a| is, the narrower the parabola; the closer |a| is to zero, the wider the parabola.

statement.p: Describe how changing the value of b affects the graph of q.

solution.p: With a = 1 and c = 0, changing b shifts the vertex diagonally: making b > 0 moves the vertex left and down, while making b < 0 moves it right and down.

statement.p: Describe how changing the value of c affects the graph of q.

solution.p: With a = 1 and b = 0, changing c moves the vertex straight up or down along the y-axis. The vertex is at (0, c), and c is the y-intercept of the function.

statement.p: Which parameter seems to have the simplest effect? Which parameter seems to have the most complicated effect? Why?

solution.p: The parameter a has the most clearly understandable effect: it controls whether the parabola opens up or down and how wide it is. The parameter c has a simple effect as well (pure vertical translation). The parameter b has the most complicated effect because changing it moves the vertex diagonally rather than purely up, down, or in terms of width.

statement.p: Is it possible to find a formula for a quadratic function that passes through the points (0,8), (1,12), (2,12)? If yes, do so; if not, explain why not.

solution.p: Yes. Since (1,12) and (2,12) have the same output, the axis of symmetry is x = 1.5 and the vertex has x-coordinate 1.5. Since the parabola must pass through (0,8), which is below the values at x=1 and x=2, the parabola opens downward. With c = q(0) = 8 and using vertex form or solving the system, the formula is q(x) = -2x^2 + 6x + 8.
